concept: Lid / Removable Cover for an Opening
description: A removable cover fitted over the opening of a container. Whether it rests loosely, seals tightly, or fastens through a mechanism, 'tapu' (close) names the action and 'tapuri' is the separate thing that meets the rim.
sound_symbolism: Dental 'ta' and stressed bilabial 'pu' make 'tapu' (close) audible before the final syllable. 'Ri' releases those two stops with a quick tongue tap and bright 'i'. The contour ends lighter and quicker than its firm opening.
grammatical_notes: A countable noun: 'ha tapuri tomi tapu.' (This lid closes the pot.) Its container precedes it as possessor when needed. Exact seals, caps, and fitted closures keep their source names when design matters.

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: The right lid kept steam in the pot, dust out of grain, and mice away from the winter store.

semantic_domains.physical: a separate object meeting the rim of a container
